title: Japanese Miso Soup

ingredients.name: white or red miso paste
visual_checks: white miso is sweeter and milder; red miso is saltier and more intense
ingredients.notes: white miso is sweeter and milder; red miso is saltier and more intense

subs.name: 1 tbsp soy sauce plus a pinch of salt for a lighter emergency broth — flavour will be simpler

ingredients.name: firm tofu, cut into 1 cm cubes

subs.name: cubed paneer (drained well) for a non-vegan version with a similar texture

ingredients.name: dried kombu (kelp), for dashi
visual_checks: a 10 cm strip is enough; use kombu labelled 'dashi kombu'
ingredients.notes: a 10 cm strip is enough; use kombu labelled 'dashi kombu'

subs.name: a strip of dried kombu from any Korean or Japanese grocery; if unavailable, simmer 1 tsp soy sauce in water for a basic umami broth
a strip of dried kombu from any Korean or Japanese grocery; if unavailable, simmer 1 tsp soy sauce in water for a basic umami broth

ingredients.name: shiitake mushrooms, whole or sliced
visual_checks: adds depth to the dashi; dried shiitake gives more umami
ingredients.notes: adds depth to the dashi; dried shiitake gives more umami

subs.name: 1 tsp instant vegetable stock powder to deepen the dashi if kombu is not available

steps.text: Combine water, the kombu piece and shiitake mushrooms (if using) in a saucepan. Place over medium-low heat and bring slowly to a bare simmer — 10 minutes. Remove the kombu just before the water reaches a full boil; leaving it in too long makes the dashi bitter.

Remove the mushrooms (slice them and return to the pot if you like). Add the dried wakame directly to the hot dashi — it rehydrates in under 2 minutes. Add the tofu cubes and let the soup return to a gentle simmer for 2 minutes.

Turn off the heat. Place the miso paste in a small ladle or fine strainer and submerge it in the soup, pressing and stirring until fully dissolved with no lumps. Never boil the soup once miso is in — heat destroys its flavour and probiotics.

Ladle into bowls immediately and scatter spring onion over the top. Serve as a starter or alongside rice and any main dish. Miso soup does not reheat well; make only as much as you will drink at one sitting.

cross_contact_notes: Ingredient metadata cannot prove an allergen-free kitchen. Check packaged labels and cross-contact statements for your products.
trust.provenance_summary: AI-assisted original draft; declared licence original.
trust.substitution_warning: A substitution can introduce allergens or change dietary suitability. Never claim a swap is safe without checking the replacement's label and the user's allergy context.

substitution_notes: tofu → cubed paneer (drained well) for a non-vegan version with a similar texture (changes diet to vegetarian); miso → 1 tbsp soy sauce plus a pinch of salt for a lighter emergency broth — flavour will be simpler (lose the fermented depth); seaweed → a strip of dried kombu from any Korean or Japanese grocery; if unavailable, simmer 1 tsp soy sauce in water for a basic umami broth (flavour will differ significantly); mushroom → 1 tsp instant vegetable stock powder to deepen the dashi if kombu is not available
indian_kitchen_adaptation: Miso paste is available at health food stores and on Amazon India (brands like Hikari or Marukome). If unavailable, substitute 1 tbsp soy sauce plus a pinch of urad dal paste dissolved in hot water for a rough umami base. Tofu is sold as 'soya paneer' or 'bean curd' in metro markets. The whole soup cooks in one saucepan with no special equipment.
